data.title: Century Communities Non-GAAP EPS of $1.59 beats by $0.20, revenue of $1.2B beats by $150M
data.description:
• Century Communities press release (CCS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.59 beats by $0.20.
  • Revenue of $1.2B (-7.7% Y/Y) beats by $150M.

  • Full Year 2026 Outlook

    Scott Dixon, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, commented, "Assuming no significant changes to the current economic environment, we currently expect our full year 2026 new home deliveries to be in the range of 10,000 to 11,000 homes and our homes sales revenues to be in the range of $3.6 billion to $4.1 billion. Our current guidance reflects an increase in our average open communities in the mid-single digit percentage range and similar per community absorption levels as the back half of 2025."

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Microsoft tops earnings, revenue expectations
Tesla Non-GAAP EPS of $0.50 beats by $0.05, revenue of $24.9B beats by $140M
data.description:
• Tesla press release (TSLA): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.50 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $24.9B (-3.1% Y/Y) beats by $140M.
  • Capex: $2.39B  vs. $2.25B in 3Q25. FCF: $1.42B vs. $3.99B in 3Q25.
  • Shares +2%.

data.title: Las Vegas Sands Non-GAAP EPS of $0.85 beats by $0.08, revenue of $3.65B beats by $310M
data.description:
• Las Vegas Sands press release (LVS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.85 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $3.65B (+25.9% Y/Y) beats by $310M.

  • Consolidated adjusted property EBITDA was $1.41 billion, compared to $1.11 billion in the prior year quarter.

    Full year 2025 operating income was $2.82 billion, compared to $2.40 billion in 2024.  Net income attributable to Las Vegas Sands (LVS) was $1.63 billion, or $2.35 per diluted share, in 2025.  This compared to $1.45 billion, or $1.96 per diluted share, in 2024.

    Sands China Ltd. Consolidated Financial ResultsOn a GAAP basis, total net revenues for SCL increased 16.4% to $2.05 billion, compared to the fourth quarter of 2024.  Net income for SCL was $213 million, compared to $237 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.

    On a GAAP basis, 2025 total net revenues for SCL increased 5.1% to $7.44 billion, compared to 2024.  Net income for SCL was $901 million in 2025, compared to $1.05 billion in 2024.

data.title: SEI Investments GAAP EPS of $1.38 beats by $0.03, revenue of $607.93M beats by $10.49M
data.description:
• SEI Investments press release (SEIC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.38 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $607.93M (+9.1% Y/Y) beats by $10.49M.
  • SEI ended the year with strong quarterly performance, achieving diluted EPS of $1.38, up 16% from the prior year. Results were broad based, driven by revenue growth and margin expansion across each of SEI's business segments. Several items affected comparability and collectively reduced diluted EPS by approximately $0.08, including:
    • $20.1 million of elevated Corporate Overhead attributable to severance expense and third party M&A fees related to Stratos.
    • A $3.0 million tax benefit from purchased energy credits.
    • A $3.3 million revenue accrual true-up benefit in our Investment Managers segment.

data.title: Meta GAAP EPS of $8.88 beats by $0.66, revenue of $59.89B beats by $1.42B
data.description:
• Meta press release (META): Q4 GAAP EPS of $8.88 beats by $0.66.
  • Revenue of $59.89B (+23.8% Y/Y) beats by $1.42B.
  • Family daily active people (DAP) – DAP was 3.58 billion on average for December 2025, an increase of 7% year-over-year.
  • Ad impressions – Ad impressions delivered across our Family of Apps increased by 18% and 12% year-over-year for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, respectively.
  • Average price per ad – Average price per ad increased by 6% and 9% year-over-year for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, respectively.

  • We expect first quarter 2026 total revenue to be in the range of $53.5-56.5 billion vs. $51.41B consensus. Our guidance assumes foreign currency is an approximately 4% tailwind to year-over-year total revenue growth, based on current exchange rates.

    We expect full year 2026 total expenses to be in the range of $162-169 billion.

  • Shares -3.5%.

data.title: QCR Holdings outlines 8–10% loan growth target for Q1 2026 while advancing digital transformation
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: QCR Holdings, Inc. (QCRH) Q4 2025

Management View

  • President and CEO Todd Gipple opened the call by stating, "We delivered our strongest quarter of the year in the fourth quarter and produced record full year results. Performance was strong across all key operating metrics, approaching or exceeding the upper end of our guidance ranges for net interest margin expansion, gross loan growth and capital markets revenue." Gipple highlighted record earnings driven by net interest margin expansion, robust loan and deposit growth, and strong capital markets revenue. He emphasized the success in all three primary lines of business: traditional banking, wealth management, and the LIHTC lending platform.
  • Gipple reported the addition of 500 new wealth management client relationships in 2025 and over $1 billion in new assets under management. He noted, "Our strong capabilities in this business have created 5-year compound annual growth rates of 10% for both assets under management and revenue."
  • The LIHTC lending business added 18 new developer partners, and Gipple announced, "We are raising the upper end of our capital markets revenue guidance, resulting in a range of $55 million to $70 million over the next 4 quarters."
  • Gipple described the sale of $285 million of LIHTC construction loans at par, stating this transaction "expands our capacity for additional permanent LIHTC lending and further enhances our opportunities for additional capital markets revenue."
  • He confirmed continued digital transformation progress, with the first of four core system conversions completed and two more planned for April and October 2026.
  • CFO Nick Anderson stated, "We delivered record adjusted net income of $37 million or $2.21 per diluted share for the quarter and record full year adjusted net income of $130 million or $7.64 per diluted share."

Outlook

  • Gipple signaled strong momentum into 2026, emphasizing a focus on digital transformation, expansion in wealth management, and continued LIHTC platform growth. He stated, "Building on our momentum and the depth of our pipeline, we are raising the upper end of our capital markets revenue guidance, resulting in a range of $55 million to $70 million over the next 4 quarters."
  • Anderson guided to "additional core margin expansion in the first quarter between 3 to 7 basis points, assuming no further federal rate cuts." He projected noninterest expenses for Q1 2026 in the range of $55 million to $58 million, assuming capital markets revenue and loan growth are within guided ranges.
  • Anderson also provided guidance for gross annualized loan growth in Q1 2026 of 8% to 10%, with growth ramping up to 10% to 15% for the remainder of the year. He expects the effective tax rate to be in the range of 8% to 10% for Q1 2026.

Financial Results

  • Anderson reported net interest income increased $4 million or 22% annualized in Q4 and $23 million or 10% for the year. Net interest margin increased by 6 basis points from the third quarter. Noninterest income totaled $39 million in Q4, driven by $25 million in capital markets revenue.
  • Wealth Management delivered $5 million in revenue for Q4 and grew $2 million or 11% for the year. Core noninterest expenses increased $4 million in Q4, with an additional $2 million nonrecurring prepayment fee.
  • Total loans grew by $304 million or 17% annualized before the impact of the construction loan sale. Total criticized loans declined $5 million in the quarter and $20 million for the year. The allowance for credit losses to total loans increased to 1.26%.
  • The tangible common equity to tangible assets ratio rose by 27 basis points to 10.24%. Common equity Tier 1 ratio increased 18 basis points to 10.52% and total risk-based capital ratio increased 16 basis points to 14.19%. Tangible book value per share rose $2.08 to approximately $58.

Q&A

  • Damon Del Monte, KBW, asked about seasonality in capital markets revenue and expectations for Q1. Gipple responded, "Our first quarter is historically our slowest quarter of the year for capital markets revenue...our Q1 capital markets revenue has averaged $11 million in the past 5 years."
  • Del Monte inquired about the timing and size of LIHTC loan securitizations. Gipple said, "We do continue to target sometime in the first half of this year. I expect us to have that perm loan securitization happen prior to June 30...something in the $300 million to $350 million range."
  • Nathan Race, Piper Sandler, asked about earning assets. Gipple stated, "Earning assets heading into the first quarter would be very consistent with where we ended earning assets."
  • Race sought clarity on expense run rates. Anderson explained, "About $4 million of digital transformation spend, another $4 million in salary benefit costs and a couple of million in occupancy related."
  • Race asked about deposit growth. Gipple discussed strategies for core deposit and mix improvement, while Anderson provided a breakdown of deposit growth sources.
  • Daniel Tamayo, Raymond James, asked about LIHTC business growth and efficiency. Gipple highlighted the need for all three business lines to contribute to ROAA improvement.
  • Brian Martin, Janney, asked about noninterest-bearing deposit targets. Gipple said, "I would certainly expect us over time to move that up to be more peer like, something in the high teens and maybe even 20%. That is not going to happen in a couple of quarters."
  • Ryan Payne, D.A. Davidson, asked about competition in LIHTC. Gipple stated, "We only have around 2% of the market...the only time we really end up losing deals is when the equity provider to that developer also has either an in-house perm loan or a relationship with someone on the perm side."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, with detailed questions on growth, margin, competition, and operational expenses. Queries were probing but not adversarial.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, and in Q&A, Gipple and Anderson were detailed and transparent about guidance, growth prospects, and operational strategy. Gipple used phrases such as "we feel really good about loan pipelines" and "we expect our growth to be funded with core deposits, not wholesale."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment remained confident, with increased emphasis on digital transformation and scalable growth. Analysts continued to focus on the sustainability of growth and capital allocation strategy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company raised the upper end of capital markets revenue guidance from $65 million to $70 million. Loan growth guidance for Q1 shifted to a more measured 8% to 10%, with acceleration expected in later quarters versus a prior 10% to 15% annualized range for Q4 2025.
  • Management reported significant progress in digital transformation, with the first core system conversion completed, compared to foundational work in the previous quarter.
  • Noninterest expense growth guidance increased to a $55 million to $58 million range, reflecting both digital investments and infrastructure for surpassing $10 billion in assets. The efficiency ratio rose slightly to 56.8% from 55.8%.
  • Analysts continued to focus on guidance, capital markets revenue durability, loan growth, and expense run rates. Management's tone remained confident with expanded detail around operational strategy.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged seasonality in capital markets revenue, particularly in Q1, and acknowledged the lengthy and evolving securitization process with Freddie Mac.
  • Gipple noted the challenge of increasing noninterest-bearing deposits and the time required for meaningful improvement in the funding mix.
  • The impact of further digital transformation costs and the need to manage expense growth while scaling were highlighted. Management emphasized ongoing work to improve operating leverage and efficiency.
  • In LIHTC, competition from equity providers with in-house lending was cited as the main challenge to winning new deals.

Final Takeaway

QCR Holdings delivered record earnings in Q4 and for the full year 2025, driven by robust growth across all core business lines. Management outlined a strategy focused on continued digital transformation, disciplined expense management, expanding wealth management, and scaling the LIHTC platform, with guidance for 8% to 10% gross annualized loan growth in Q1 2026 and raised capital markets revenue targets. The team remains confident in delivering sustainable profitability and shareholder value through operational improvements and a balanced approach to growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Bessent says Trump's Fed chair announcement may come in 'next week or so'
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Wednesday that President Donald Trump's final pick for Federal Reserve chair could be announced "in the next week or so."

His remarks during an interview with Yahoo Finance suggest a modest delay from earlier remarks that had pointed to a decision as soon as late January.

Prediction markets have BlackRock (BLK) executive Rick Rieder in the lead to take the Fed chair seat in May 2026, when current chief Jerome Powell's term ends. Former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh stood in second place, while Fed Governor Christopher Waller rounded out the top three candidates.

Powell is facing an ongoing criminal probe over the central bank's renovation of its Washington headquarters and whether he lied to Congress about the project’s scope during his congressional testimony last June. Powell has said the investigation is a politically motivated pretext tied to pressure on the Fed over interest rates.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Alkami Technology jumps after report it's exploring a potential sale
data.description:
Alkami Technology (ALKT) soared 7.4% after a report that it's exploring options, including a possible sale.

Alkami (ALKT) is working with a financial adviser to garner interest from possible bidders, according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Strategic buyers and private equity shops are expected to be interested. 

Deliberations are ongoing, and there's no certainty a deal will transpire, according to the report. Alkami didn't respond to Bloomberg requests for comment. Alkami has a market cap of $2.2 billion.

A potential sale comes amid pressure from activist investor Jana Partners, which last month said the banking technology company is undervalued and should explore a sale to a rival or private equity firm.
